entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The main agricultural crop studied for soil pest infestation in West Africa

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where the study on groundnut soil pests was conducted

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: One of the West African countries where groundnut farms were surveyed

entities.name: BURKINA-FASO
entities.description: One of the West African countries where groundnut farms were surveyed

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: One of the West African countries where groundnut farms were surveyed

entities.name: NIGERIA
entities.description: One of the West African countries where groundnut farms were surveyed

entities.name: TERMITES
entities.description: Soil arthropods, specifically of the genus Microtermes, that attack groundnut plants and kernels

entities.name: MICROTERMES
entities.description: A genus of termites found to be the most abundant and widely distributed species of economic importance

entities.name: WHITEGRUB
entities.description: Soil pests from the family Scarabaeidae, specifically the genus Schyzonycha, that infest groundnut farms

entities.name: SCHYZONYCHA
entities.description: A genus of whitegrubs identified in the surveyed groundnut fields

entities.name: MILLIPEDE
entities.description: Soil pests from the family Odontopygidae, specifically the genus Peridontopyge, that infest groundnut farms

entities.name: PERIDONTOPYGE
entities.description: A genus of millipedes identified in the surveyed groundnut fields

entities.name: YIELD LOSS
entities.description: The reduction in groundnut harvest resulting from pest damage, particularly by termites

entities.name: SPECIES DIVERSITY
entities.description: The variety and distribution of different soil pest species in groundnut farms

entities.name: CEREAL CROP RESIDUES
entities.description: Remnants of previous cereal crops in the fields that facilitate the spread of termites

entities.name: PLANT MATURITY
entities.description: The growth stage of the groundnut crop where pest population densities and observation frequencies change

affects the frequency of observation on plants
